description:
You are a Solar Energy Systems Engineer tasked with mitigating the 'Duck Curve' for a utility-scale solar project.

The Energy3D project '/home/ga/Documents/Energy3D/duck_curve_starter.ng3' is open and contains a standard, monolithic South-facing solar array. Your goal is to flatten its daily generation profile to reduce the noon spike and boost late-afternoon power production.

Instructions:
1. Change the location to 'Los Angeles, CA' and the date to the Autumnal Equinox (September 21).
2. Run the Daily Yield Analysis (Analysis > Solar Panels > Daily Yield Analysis) for the baseline design. Note the generation values at 12:00 (peak) and 16:00 (late afternoon).
3. Redesign the installation: split the monolithic array into three separate sections (or rotate existing racks) to create a multi-azimuth array:
   - ~1/3 of the panels facing East (Azimuth ~90°)
   - ~1/3 of the panels facing South (Azimuth ~180°)
   - ~1/3 of the panels facing West (Azimuth ~270°)
   (Keep the total number of solar panels roughly equal to the baseline).
4. Run the Daily Yield Analysis again for the new design on September 21.
5. Take a screenshot of the new Daily Yield graph (showing the flattened curve) and save it as '~/Documents/Energy3D/screenshot_daily_yield.png'.
6. Create a text report at '~/Documents/Energy3D/profile_comparison.txt' reporting four values (in kWh) clearly labeled:
   - Baseline yield at 12:00
   - Baseline yield at 16:00
   - New yield at 12:00
   - New yield at 16:00
7. Save the modified 3D model as '~/Documents/Energy3D/duck_curve_mitigated.ng3'.
